factors.name: SpaceX IPO & Tech Momentum
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO closed +19% on Friday, becoming 6th most-valuable US company ($2T+ valuation implied). Tech strength historically supports Dow's MSFT, GE, HON exposure and signals broad risk appetite.

factors.name: US-Iran Deal Progress
factors.summary: Trump announced US-Iran peace deal to be signed Sunday; Pakistan sources suggest Hormuz reopening within 24h. Geopolitical de-escalation removes risk premium from oil/energy, supports global growth narrative.

factors.name: Macro Data Vacuum & Tariff Uncertainty
factors.summary: No ISM Manufacturing/Services PMI, durable goods, or CPI data in 24h window. Morgan Stanley reports limited evidence of reshoring after one year of tariffs, suggesting trade policy is weighing on capex and industrial sentiment.

factors.name: National Debt & Fiscal Headwinds
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports US national debt hit $100T (400% of GDP), $1M per household. Social Security 2032 cliff at 22% cut looming. Fiscal deterioration typically pressures long-end yields and dividend-dependent Dow plays.

factors.name: AI Regulatory Headwinds
factors.summary: Anthropic suspended Claude Fable 5 over US government cybersecurity concerns; Amazon voiced concerns pre-crackdown. Regulatory friction on AI may slow capex at MSFT, GS, and broader tech suppliers to Dow names.

factors.name: Rate Expectations & Yield Stability
factors.summary: ECB's Nagel expects prices to stay elevated even if Iran war ends; Fed/BOE staying 'guarded.' No major rate surprise in latest headlines; nominal/real yield trajectory unclear, limiting conviction on rate-sensitive dividend payers.

factors.name: Recent Price Action (4h: -0.07%, 24h: +0.30%)
factors.summary: Dow up only 30bp in last 24h despite SpaceX IPO and Iran deal optimism. Muted moves into weekend suggest consolidation or cautious positioning ahead of major catalysts; no established trend momentum.

factors.name: China Growth Data & Export Risk
factors.summary: No recent China PMI or GDP data in news window. Tariff uncertainty and reshoring weakness suggest China growth headwinds; CAT, BA, industrial exports under pressure from policy and slowing demand.

factors.name: Earnings Season Timing
factors.summary: No major Dow heavyweight earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA) reported in 24h window. Forward guidance vacuum limits conviction until earnings resume or Fed commentary shifts.

factors.name: Credit Spreads & Private Credit Stress
factors.summary: Korean media conglomerate JTBC defaulted on securitized loans (junk downgrade). Private credit stress signals potential spillover to broad corporate health; CLO ETF boom may mask underlying credit risks.

screening.narrative: The Dow is trapped in a consolidation zone despite positive geopolitical catalysts (Iran deal, SpaceX IPO, tech momentum) and structural fiscal headwinds (debt surge, Social Security cliff, tariff uncertainty). The 4h/24h price action shows minimal directional conviction: +0.30% in 24h is too weak to suggest accumulation despite SpaceX's historic debut. Regulatory friction on AI and lack of fresh macro data (ISM, CPI, employment) leave the market in wait-and-see mode heading into the weekend and the week ahead. Fiscal deterioration and China growth concerns are submerged but not resolved.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision (date TBD, likely next week) – major rate guidance shift could flip bias
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI – direct read on Dow industrial/services composition
US-Iran peace deal signing (claimed Sunday) – oil/energy and geopolitical risk premium reset
Dow heavyweight earnings resume – UNH, MSFT, GS, JPM, CAT forward guidance
China PMI & growth data – CAT, BA, materials demand signal
CPI/PCE inflation print – input cost pressures for industrials, rate expectations
screening.risk_warning: A failed or delayed US-Iran deal could reverse geopolitical relief and spike oil/VIX. Hawkish Fed surprise or hot inflation print would invalidate the cautious-bullish SpaceX narrative and pressure dividend-heavy Dow names. Conversely, dovish pivot or stronger China stimulus could catalyze FOMO rally that invalidates consolidation setup.
screening.entry_guidance: Consolidation pattern dominates; avoid chasing. On break above 51,500, re-test for confirmation of bullish breakout. Below 50,850, watch for support hold. Best entry is pullback to 51,000-51,100 within consolidation range before major catalyst (FOMC/ISM) clarity.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO & Tech Momentum
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO closed +19% on Friday, transforming into 6th most-valuable US company and lifting market sentiment after volatile week. Tech/aerospace enthusiasm may provide near-term tailwind for Dow industrials (BA exposure).

factors.name: Iran-US Deal Narrative
factors.summary: Trump claims peace deal signing Sunday; Iran disputes timing ('will not be tomorrow'). Geopolitical de-escalation would lower oil/commodity risk premiums, but uncertainty remains unresolved—no definitive catalyst yet.

factors.name: Strait of Hormuz Skirmishes
factors.summary: Ongoing friction near strategic waterway despite peace talks; Pakistan reported deal could finalize within 24h but risk remains elevated. Oil price volatility and supply-chain anxiety could weigh on industrials and transportation (CVX, BA, CAT).

factors.name: USD Strength & DXY
factors.summary: Dollar 'likely to find support as long as Strait remains mostly closed' per market commentary; Dow has ~50% international revenue, so strong USD is a headwind for multinational translation. No new USD data in window.

factors.name: Real Yields & Fed Policy
factors.summary: Fed 'staying guarded' per Bloomberg after 100 days of Iran war; no new FOMC decision or major rate/inflation surprise in news window. Central bank messaging remains cautious, not supportive of equity rally.

factors.name: Inflation Risk & Commodity Pressure
factors.summary: Gold 'under pressure since US/Israel launched war against Iran in late February' per Al Jazeera; ECB's Nagel warns 'prices likely to stay higher even if conflict ends.' Input-cost inflation remains sticky for Dow industrials (CAT, BA, MMM).

factors.name: Manufacturing & Cyclical Outlook
factors.summary: No new ISM PMI, durable goods, or factory orders data in window. Last bias (2026-06-14 00:05) was NEUTRAL 4/10 with no confirmation. Lack of fresh hard data prevents decisive cyclical read.

factors.name: Corporate Earnings & Guidance
factors.summary: No major Dow heavyweight earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA) released in news window. Absence of guidance updates leaves forward confidence weak.

factors.name: Equity Market Breadth & Sentiment
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO enthusiasm offset by broader geopolitical/inflation anxiety. Knicks NBA win (cultural sentiment) is noise; South Korea market upside chasing MSCI upgrade is Asia-specific, not US30 driver.

factors.name: Price Action Flatness
factors.summary: 4h change -0.07%, 24h change +0.30% — essentially no directional conviction. Index near all-time highs, vulnerable to profit-taking or reversal if headlines deteriorate.

screening.narrative: The Dow is pinned in tight consolidation, with SpaceX's blockbuster IPO (+19%) providing short-term enthusiasm for tech/aerospace exposure but offset by persistent geopolitical risk (Iran deal uncertainty, Strait of Hormuz skirmishes) and sticky inflation concerns that weigh on industrial input costs. Fed remains guarded, no major economic data has surprised in either direction over the past 24h, and Dow heavyweights have not released guidance to confirm forward cyclical strength. The index lacks conviction and is vulnerable to headline shock.
catalysts_ahead: Iran-US peace deal signing (claimed Sunday, but Iran disputes timing — could trigger ±200 bp if agreed or if talks collapse)
ISM Manufacturing PMI & ISM Services PMI (next scheduled release, likely early to mid-week)
Durable Goods Orders & Factory Orders (weekly/monthly data, critical for Dow industrials confidence)
Fed speakers (ongoing, any hawkish tone could trigger reversal)
Oil prices & Strait of Hormuz updates (daily risk)
Next Dow component earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA guidance window)
screening.risk_warning: A confirmed Iran-US deal signed with clear Strait reopening terms would de-risk oil/commodities and trigger immediate +500–1000 bp rally in equities (BULLISH flip). Conversely, breakdown in peace talks, new Middle East military escalation, or surprise CPI/PCE print >3.5% would invalidate current NEUTRAL and trigger sharp selloff into resistance.
screening.entry_guidance: Index is flat and choppy with no directional trigger. Do not chase; wait for either: (1) Iran deal signed + oil rallies past $70 for LONG entry near 51,150 with target 51,400, or (2) geopolitical flare or inflation surprise for SHORT entry near 51,300 with target 50,900. Currently, bias is too weak to initiate.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO & Tech Sentiment
factors.summary: SpaceX closed +19% on Friday ($75B IPO), transforming sentiment after a volatile week. This reflects strong risk appetite and confidence in AI/growth narratives, benefiting Dow's tech holdings (MSFT) and broad equity sentiment.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Proximity
factors.summary: Trump signaled a deal could be signed Sunday; Pakistan reports finalization within 24 hours. Peace/normalization would reduce geopolitical premium on oil and volatility, supporting equities. However, Iran disputes timing, creating uncertainty.

factors.name: Gold Weakness & De-Risk Flows
factors.summary: Gold under pressure since US-Iran war began in late February; as deal prospects rise, safe-haven demand fades. This suggests risk-on rotation INTO equities rather than defensive positioning, but move is gradual, not a sudden flush.

factors.name: Fed & Rate Expectations (Guarded Stance)
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports Fed and BOE remaining guarded after 100 days of Iran war, uncertain whether war poses greater risk to inflation or growth. No hawkish or dovish signals in news; no recent FOMC or CPI data provided. Rate outlook remains undefined.

factors.name: Near-Term Economic Data Gaps
factors.summary: No major ISM, durable goods, CPI, or employment data releases in this news window. This is a lightweight data week; without fresh hard numbers on manufacturing PMI, inflation, or jobs, macro conviction is limited.

factors.name: Dow Heavyweight Earnings / Forward Guidance
factors.summary: No earnings beats, misses, or forward guidance from major Dow names (UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA) mentioned in the past 24h. Recent news focused on smaller-cap moves (Intel double-upgrade, Celldex Phase 1 data).

factors.name: USD Strength / FX Translation
factors.summary: No commentary on dollar strength, DXY levels, or currency moves in provided news. Dow has ~50% international revenue exposure; absence of USD weakness/strength signals leaves this factor unmoored.

factors.name: China Growth & Stimulus
factors.summary: No China PMI, GDP, or policy news in this window. This is a major omission for CAT, BA, and broader cyclical conviction. Mongolia's reaffirmation of One China policy is geopolitical noise, not economic data.

factors.name: Oil / Energy Complex
factors.summary: Oil prices not mentioned; UK seizure of Russian shadow fleet tanker is supply-side chatter, not a macro demand signal. Without crude levels, energy input-cost pressure on Dow industrials cannot be assessed.

factors.name: Volatility & Risk Sentiment (VIX Proxy)
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO success and Iran deal chatter suggest risk-on mood, but Dow's 4h change of -0.07% and 24h +0.30% are near-flat—no volatility spike. VIX levels not reported; implied volatility appears muted pre-weekend.

screening.narrative: The Dow is treading water near 51,202 as the market balances two competing narratives: (1) renewed risk appetite from SpaceX's landmark IPO and proximity to a US-Iran peace deal, which supports equity sentiment; (2) a near-total absence of fresh hard economic data (ISM, jobs, inflation) and uncertainty around Fed policy, which limits upside conviction. The 4h and 24h moves are negligible, signaling indecision. This is a classic pre-weekend, low-volume tape where macro clarity is absent.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran peace deal signature (claimed Sunday by Trump, but timing disputed)
ISM Manufacturing PMI (if released next week)
ISM Services PMI (if released next week)
Jobless Claims / NFP preview data
CPI or PCE inflation print (date TBD)
Dow component earnings (HD, GS, etc. — none imminent per news)
Fed speakers (Barr, Powell, et al. — risk of hawkish/dovish surprises)
China PMI releases (weeks ahead, critical for CAT/BA demand)
screening.risk_warning: This bias could flip sharply if: (1) Iran deal falls apart or is postponed, reigniting geopolitical premium and oil spike; (2) a surprise CPI/PCE print comes in hot, forcing Fed repricing and equity selloff; (3) China disappoints on growth data, triggering cyclical rout; (4) Dow heavyweight earnings miss or cut guidance; (5) dollar rallies hard on rate repricing, hurting multinational translation. The absence of data AND the weekend timing make this a low-confidence read—chop and reversals are high-probability.
screening.entry_guidance: Do not chase any direction aggressively; this is a consolidation day ahead of weekend/early-week catalysts. If bias eventually turns BULLISH on Iran deal finalization + SpaceX momentum, consider buying only on a retest of 51,000 support. If BEARISH catalyst (CPI or deal collapse) emerges, short the 51,500 resistance. Current price is in the middle of the range; wait for a break and retest before committing.

factors.name: Price Action Momentum
factors.summary: 4h change -0.07%, 24h change +0.30% indicates choppy, directionless trading with minimal net movement. No sustained conviction in either direction.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Sentiment Spillover
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO surged 19% on first day, closing at $75B valuation, and lifted broader market sentiment after volatile week. Musk's success and Starship narrative may support risk appetite in tech-heavy Dow components (MSFT, etc.), though this is indirect.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk – US-Iran Deal Narrative
factors.summary: Trump claims US-Iran peace deal signing on Sunday; Iran disputes timing and remains cautious. Simultaneous Israel-Hezbollah escalation (Beirut strikes) and uncertainty on deal completion create conflicting narratives. Risk-off potential if deal falls apart; risk-on if resolved.

factors.name: Earnings & Forward Guidance (Dow Heavyweights)
factors.summary: No major Dow component earnings or guidance released in the past 24h. Lack of fresh catalysts from UNH, MSFT, GS, JPM, BA, CAT, etc. reduces near-term directional conviction.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: No FOMC decision or Fed commentary in recent news. No major shift in rate expectations signaled. Absence of inflation data (CPI/PCE) in latest window limits pivot signals.

factors.name: Equity Valuation & Technicals (Dow-Specific)
factors.summary: Dow remains near all-time highs (51.2K level). SpaceX IPO enthusiasm did not trigger a fresh breakout; price is consolidating. No clear technical confirmation of new uptrend initiation.

factors.name: Macro Soft Data – Consumer & Confidence
factors.summary: Reuters/Ipsos poll shows Trump support slipping in rural America due to climbing fuel and food prices. Consumer mood erosion is a subtle headwind for Dow consumer names (HD, DIS, KO proxy exposure) and inflation persistence signals.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Multinational Translation
factors.summary: No fresh DXY or currency data in past 24h. Dollar momentum is unclear. Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure remains exposed to FX headwinds if USD rallies, but no new catalyst evident.

factors.name: China PMI & Growth Data
factors.summary: No recent China PMI or economic releases in the news window. Absence of data limits bearish momentum from growth concerns.

factors.name: Inflation & Input Costs (CPI/PPI/PCE Missing)
factors.summary: No CPI, PCE, or PPI releases in past 24h. Without fresh inflation data, no clear signal on cost pressures for Dow industrials (CAT, MMM, BA, etc.).

screening.narrative: The Dow remains in a narrow consolidation after SpaceX's successful IPO (19% pop) provided a brief sentiment boost on Friday. However, overnight trading shows minimal follow-through (+0.30% in 24h, -0.07% in 4h), suggesting traders are waiting for fresh catalysts. Geopolitical uncertainty around the US-Iran deal (Trump claims Sunday signing, Iran disputes) and rural consumer weakness (fuel/food price inflation) are creating mixed crosscurrents. Absence of major earnings, Fed guidance, or inflation data keeps bias neutral.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran deal signing (Trump says Sunday, but timing unclear and Iran skeptical)
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (major Dow industrial sentiment indicator)
Durable goods orders & factory orders
Upcoming Dow heavyweight earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, JPM, BA, CAT, HD timeline TBD)
Fed speakers / rate expectations updates
Treasury yield moves (10Y, 2Y) affecting rate-sensitive Dow names

screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran deal collapses or escalates (Israel-Hezbollah tensions spike), geopolitical risk-off could trigger sharp selloff. Conversely, deal success could unlock risk rally and break Dow through 51.5K resistance. CPI or hawkish Fed surprise would invert bias sharply bearish. Industrial earnings misses would confirm demand erosion hinted at by fuel/food inflation complaints.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is NEUTRAL with low conviction. Avoid chasing; wait for either (a) fresh earnings/PMI data to confirm direction, (b) US-Iran deal clarity (bullish on signing, bearish on failure), or (c) retest of 50,850 support for long entry or 51,500 resistance for short setup. Consolidation likely to persist until next week's data.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Kevin Warsh Era
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Warsh facing debut press conference amid inflation uncertainty and reported division within the Fed (Bloomberg 2026-06-14T13:49). No clear directional signal yet; market pricing depends on Warsh's communications stance, but major catalyst coming.

factors.name: Equity Valuations & Stretched Multiples
factors.summary: Pimco warns defaults in debt markets are starting again and equity valuations look stretched; recommends fixed income to anchor portfolios (MarketWatch 2026-06-14T13:24). SpaceX IPO surge (+19% first day, Musk becomes first trillionaire) may signal euphoria peak, but AI growth story hinges on lower rates—which higher Fed policy could threaten.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk & US-Iran Deal
factors.summary: Compressed timeline on US-Iran nuclear deal creates uncertainty; Trump claims signing Sunday but Tehran disputes timing (Al Jazeera 2026-06-14T11:18). Israel attacking Beirut to derail deal, G7 summit overshadowed by talks (Bloomberg 2026-06-14T12:18). Escalation risk could spike VIX and weigh on risk assets.

factors.name: Dow Heavyweight Earnings & Forward Guidance
factors.summary: No recent earnings surprises or guidance revisions cited in news window. Upcoming week includes BoJ, RBA, BoE decisions and US retail sales/ADP employment—critical for Dow's cyclical/financial exposure but data not yet released.

factors.summary: BBC notes US economy continues to outperform peers despite global shocks (BBC 2026-06-14T05:14), suggesting underlying strength. However, no fresh hard data (CPI, NFP, retail sales) in this window to confirm momentum.

factors.name: Interest Rates & Long-End Yields
factors.summary: No 10Y or long-end yield data in news window. Fed pivot expectations unclear pending Warsh guidance. Rate-sensitive Dow components (utilities, REITs, financials) lack directional catalyst.

factors.summary: BoJ, RBA, BoE decisions imminent this week; diverging rate paths forcing emerging-market reshuffle (Bloomberg 2026-06-14T12:30). Fed tightening bias vs. other central banks could benefit DXY and multinational Dow earnings translation, but unclear until announcements.

factors.name: Trade & China Growth
factors.summary: No fresh China PMI, industrial production, or trade data in window. Trump tariff/trade policy commentary sparse in recent news. Dow exposure to China demand (CAT, BA) lacks concrete catalyst direction.

factors.summary: No oil price data or CVX-specific guidance in news. Geopolitical tensions (Iran, Middle East) could support oil volatility, but no spot price move documented.

factors.name: Credit Spreads & CLO/Default Risk
factors.summary: Pimco explicitly warns defaults in debt markets starting again; CLO ETFs boom on elevated rates but signal private debt stress (Bloomberg 2026-06-13T18:09). Spread widening would pressurize Dow financials and growth names.

screening.narrative: The Dow remains pinned in neutral after three consecutive NEUTRAL reads today, reflecting the collision of conflicting macro narratives: US economic resilience and equity valuations that are stretched per Pimco, balanced against geopolitical escalation risk (Iran deal uncertainty, Beirut strikes), a new Fed Chair's unproven messaging, and early warning signs of credit stress. SpaceX's blockbuster IPO may reflect AI/growth euphoria at peak, but higher rates threaten that story. No major economic data has moved the index decisively in the 4h (+−0.07%) or 24h (+0.30%), suggesting the market is in a wait-and-see posture ahead of this week's BoJ, RBA, BoE, and US retail sales/ADP releases.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Chair Warsh press conference (imminent, this week) — tone on inflation/rates critical
BoJ policy decision (Tuesday 2026-06-17) — rate differential vs. Fed
RBA policy decision (Tuesday 2026-06-17) — global rate divergence signal
BoE decision (upcoming, likely mid-week) — European growth spillover
US ADP employment change & retail sales (week of 2026-06-15) — domestic demand signal
US industrial production (May, expected this week) — Dow cyclical input
G7 Summit (2026-06-15–17) & Iran deal signing (claimed Sunday 2026-06-15, unconfirmed) — geopolitical resolution or escalation
Defaults data / credit spread widening confirmation — Pimco warning
screening.risk_warning: If Warsh signals dovish pivot or Fed rate cuts → equities rally hard, Dow could spike 1–2% to 51600+. Conversely, if Iran deal collapses or Israel/Hezbollah escalates, VIX spikes and Dow reprices lower to 50700–50800. CPI/PCE surprise hot mid-week would flip bias BEARISH and threaten 50500 support. Positive US jobs/retail surprise could support 51500+ but currently unconfirmed.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is NEUTRAL with low confidence (4/10); avoid aggressive directional positions until either Warsh guidance clarifies Fed stance or Iran deal resolves. If Dow retests 51000 on geopolitical panic, consider nibble-long entry for mean reversion. If Warsh sounds hawkish, look for retest of 51400 resistance as rejection signal for short. Wait for week's economic data before conviction build.

factors.summary: Pimco warns defaults in debt markets are starting; notes equity valuations look stretched and recommends fixed income. S&P 500 warned another 3% drop could trigger systematic selling—Dow would follow.

factors.summary: BBC notes US economy 'keeps defying the odds' despite global shocks. Underlying data supports continuation, though valuations stretched per Pimco warning.

Fed Chair Warsh's debut press conference this week is a pivotal unknown; Pimco's stretch-valuation warning and systematic-selling threshold (another 3% S&P drop) add overhead risk. With no major Dow component earnings or economic data in the immediate window, the bias stays anchored to event risk rather than fundamentals.

factors.summary: SpaceX $75B IPO and AI rally have driven up cost of equity funding. Bloomberg reports 'equity funding squeeze' and leveraged ETF growth straining market plumbing—potential liquidity headwind but not immediately directional for Dow.

factors.name: Pimco Caution on Defaults & Stretched Equity Valuations
factors.summary: Pimco warns 'defaults in debt markets are starting again' and recommends fixed income to 'anchor portfolios as equity valuations look stretched.' BofA notes another 3% S&P 500 drop may trigger systematic selling.

factors.name: G7 Summit (June 15-17) & Trump Attendance
factors.summary: G7 summit in France; Trump was initially absent due to UFC event scheduling, now attending. Summit overshadowed by Iran deal uncertainty; unlikely to produce material policy surprises this early week.

factors.name: Week Ahead: ISM PMI, Retail Sales, ADP Jobs
factors.summary: Multiple economic releases scheduled (BoJ, RBA, BoE, US Industrial Production, US ADP employment, EZ ZEW Survey) provide data risk but no hard actuals yet. Data-dependent flows likely.

factors.name: US Domestic Risk (Post-Knicks Riots, Senator Hospitalization)
factors.summary: Knicks NBA title sparked celebratory riots in NYC (63 arrests, bus fires). Sen. Mitch McConnell hospitalized Sunday. Domestic noise; minimal direct market impact but adds to risk-off sentiment backdrop.

factors.name: China Economic Data (Pending)
factors.summary: Chinese Industrial Production and Retail Sales for May due this week; Caterpillar and Boeing sensitive to China demand. No recent data yet; awaiting release.

factors.name: Dollar & Commodity Price Action
factors.summary: No major FX or commodity moves noted in news window. Oil prices not highlighted; dollar strength/weakness not yet a dominant theme. Energy (CVX) and materials demand remain data-dependent.

screening.narrative: The Dow remains stuck in a narrow range (+0.30% over 24h, -0.07% over 4h) as markets grapple with heightened geopolitical tail risk from Israel-Iran escalation and an uncertain US-Iran nuclear deal signing expected this week. SpaceX's blockbuster IPO and AI rally have inflated equity valuations and strained market funding conditions, while Pimco and BofA warn of stretched valuations and potential systematic selling triggers. Domestically, the Fed's new Chair Warsh debuts this week, and a slate of economic releases (ISM, retail sales, jobs data) loom, creating both data risk and a bias toward near-term chop rather than directional conviction.
catalysts_ahead: Kevin Warsh first Fed press conference (timing TBD, likely mid-week)
G7 Summit (June 15–17, France) — Iran deal signaling key
US Industrial Production (May) — Monday June 15
BoJ, RBA, BoE policy announcements (June 15–17)
US ADP Employment Change (weekly, during week)
German ZEW Sentiment Survey (June, early week)
Chinese Industrial Production & Retail Sales (May, during week)
screening.risk_warning: If Israel-Iran tensions de-escalate and US-Iran deal signs as expected, risk-on sentiment could reverse this NEUTRAL bias sharply to BULLISH; conversely, if talks collapse or Iran retaliates militarily, equities face a swift risk-off washout. Also watch: any surprise hot inflation data, or dovish Warsh guidance, could shift the rate/valuation narrative. Confidence remains low pending concrete catalysts and resolution of headline geopolitical uncertainty.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid aggressive entries into this narrow consolidation until either: (1) geopolitical clarity emerges (Iran deal signed or talks collapse), or (2) Warsh press conference signals Fed policy direction. If US30 breaks above 51,500 on strong volume, test resistance; if it breaks below 50,900, watch for support at 50,700–50,800. Better entry on pullback to support with confirmation.
